reasons: scripts/lib/trim.sh defines trim() using ${value#"${value%%[!$whitespace]*}"} / ${value%"${value##*[!$whitespace]}"} which removes leading/trailing spaces and tabs (class $' \t\r\n\v\f') and handles empty/all-whitespace inputs correctly (criterion 1).
Only prefix/suffix trimming is performed, so interior whitespace is preserved; tests/trim.sh asserts 'a  b' -> 'a  b' (criterion 2).
tests/trim.sh sources scripts/lib/trim.sh and asserts all three required cases: '  hi  ' -> 'hi', 'a  b' -> 'a  b', '' -> '' (criterion 3).
tests/trim.sh runs under set -euo pipefail so any failed assertion exits nonzero, and it exits 0 when all pass; evidence confirms 'bash tests/trim.sh' exited 0 (criterion 4).
Changed files (scripts/lib/trim.sh, tests/trim.sh) are within in_scope globs; evidence reports integrity PASS and scope PASS.
